title: QUIC RFC 9000 Path Validation Response and Retry Work Item
summary: Carve direct proof and permanent benchmark coverage for the supported runtime path-validation response and retry floor without broadening migration semantics.
planned_changes:
- Add a focused architecture, work item, and verification artifact set for path-validation response and retry behavior.
- Link the involved RFC 9000 requirements to the focused artifact set while preserving the broad RFC9000-0001 trace.
- Add deterministic fuzz topoffs for timer-driven PATH_CHALLENGE retry payload changes and active-path PATH_RESPONSE one-shot behavior.
- Add a permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for PATH_CHALLENGE generation/formatting, PATH_RESPONSE formatting, and the candidate-path retry transition.
- Preserve focused verification evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-path-validation-response-retry/`.
verification_plan: Run the focused RFC 9000 path-validation requirement-home tests, run existing PATH_CHALLENGE/PATH_RESPONSE frame-codec fuzz tests, run the new `QuicPathValidationBenchmarks` BenchmarkDotNet suite with the Dry job, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, and preserve evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-path-validation-response-retry/`.
out_of_scope: Changing PATH_RESPONSE into a retransmitted sender-ledger packet.
Broader sender pacing or recovery policy.
completion_notes: Completed as a proof, fuzz, and benchmark topoff over the existing runtime path-validation response and retry branch; no production behavior changed.
